checks.finding: ## Architecture (design.md lines 19-38) mermaid flowchart names top-level component boundaries (loop-inventory.json, four new suites, loop-driver) and integration edges into existing components (IT -->|derive surfaces, cross-check| REPO = plugins/**/scripts/*-review-precheck.sh + validate-review-context-set.sh; LD -->|drive read-only| REAL = real prechecks/cycle-limit/select-agent-model/terminal-tier scripts; LC -.->|SDD_LOOP_REPO_ROOT override| PRE = 2d8c6a5^ worktree). Technology choices per major layer: ## Components (lines 42-51) states JSON for the inventory, Bash/PowerShell for driver+suites, GitHub Actions YAML for CI; ## Layer Specifications (lines 79-84) explicitly marks Frontend/UX N/A with rationale (no GUI) and states Infrastructure = CI 3-OS matrix/deterministic lane, Security = fixture isolation. All three required elements present.

Dependency graph extracted from ## Architecture mermaid (design.md lines 21-38): RA -> {IT,DT,LC,LE}; IT -> {REPO, INV}; LD -> {INV, REAL}; DT -> LD; LC -> {LD, PRE}; LE -> LD. This is a DAG (RA is the sole root; INV/REPO/REAL/PRE are the sole leaves); no node has a path back to any ancestor. No cycle found.

## Data Plan (design.md lines 108-118) states all three required sub-fields: Data Entities: tests/loops/loop-inventory.json plus mktemp-scoped synthetic fixtures; Existing Data Affected: none, with rationale ('Real reports/, specs/, and specs/workflow-state-registry.json are never written by the harness'); Migration Strategy: none, consistent with the stated absence of any existing-data change.

acceptance-tests.md (lines 36-37) explicitly states this is test-infrastructure work with no user-facing entry point, so the requirements-reference-user-facing-interactions trigger does not apply. ## API / Contract Plan (design.md lines 120-213) is populated, not empty: it defines the new loop-inventory/v1 JSON schema and field rules (lines 122-166) plus the tests/lib/loop-driver function contract (lines 168-198) with explicit input signatures (loop_fixture_init <profile> <feature>, drive_review_round <stage> <attempt> <round> <verdict>, etc.) and defined behavior/output per function -- the equivalent of request/response schema for this non-HTTP harness. No existing contract is deprecated or broken: the Protected-File Statement (lines 63-75) and Constraint Compliance table (lines 293-302) confirm all changes are additive new files plus non-protected registration-array edits.

## Security Boundaries (design.md lines 268-276) names three trust boundaries (B1 fixture world vs real repository, B2 harness vs real gate semantics, B3 test payloads vs hook-guard command-line analysis), states the auth/authz mechanism at each (mktemp isolation + fixture-root-outside-repo assertion; REAL binaries driven read-only with non-decreasing guarantee; protected-basename+write-verb confinement to script interiors), states data classification (synthetic fixtures / internal source, no PII), and OWASP concerns (Broken Access Control prevented, Integrity, Injection avoided by construction). Cross-referenced to security-spec.md's more detailed Trust Boundaries (lines 23-27), STRIDE (lines 31-38), and OWASP Mapping (lines 63-68) tables, which are consistent.

## Test Strategy (design.md lines 215-239) covers: unit-level negative self-checks per function (item 1, lines 217-221, explicitly naming the project's zero-mocking principle -- 'REAL binaries only'); integration-level chains (item 2 RED-differential procedure across precheck+validator, lines 222-232; item 3 escalation-leg table tests across cycle-limit/select-agent-model/terminal-tier/resume, lines 233-234); degradation-path testing (item 4, lines 235-237); and acceptance verification via concrete named commands ('bash tests/run-all.sh', 'pwsh tests/run-all.ps1', 3-OS CI matrix authoritative, item 5, lines 238-239). Each item maps to named AC/TEST IDs via acceptance-tests.md's Test ID column (TEST-001/002/007/010/012 negative self-checks, TEST-009 RED differential, TEST-011/018 escalation table, TEST-013 degradation). No fullstack E2E/user-journey path is required since Feature Type has no UI surface.

Every component named in ## Architecture (INV, IT, LD, DT, LC, LE, RA) is defined in ## Components (design.md lines 42-51). REPO and REAL (mermaid edge-label nodes for existing precheck/validator/cycle-limit/select-agent-model/terminal-tier scripts) are established existing components, itemized explicitly in the 'Real gate surfaces exercised READ-ONLY' list (lines 53-61) and corroborated by investigation.md INV-001/INV-002/INV-004/INV-014-017.
